question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the monitor to fail to detect a network shutdown by exploiting the fact that doOnError(t -> subscription.onError(t)) records errors in the subscription's error list but the onErrorResume(e -> Mono.empty()) immediately after it prevents the error from propagating, so the subscription continues to appear healthy while accumulating silent failures?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the monitor to permanently stop checking transaction confirmations by triggering an OutOfMemoryError or StackOverflowError inside the flatMap() pipeline, which is not caught by onErrorResume() (which only handles checked/reactive errors), causing the subscription to terminate abnormally?
